step1_context.flow_summary: Jesus explains the new birth to Nicodemus, culminating in the declaration of God's love and the purpose of the Son's coming.
step1_context.genre_rationale: This is a theological discourse within a narrative Gospel, where Jesus teaches Nicodemus about salvation.
step1_context.redemptive_historical_rationale: Jesus speaks of God sending His Son into the world, pointing to the incarnation and its salvific purpose.

renderings.text: For God so loved the world, that he gave his only begotten Son, that whosoever believeth in him should not perish, but have everlasting life.
For God so loved the world, that he gave his only Son, that whoever believes in him should not perish but have eternal life.
For God so loved the world that he gave his one and only Son, that whoever believes in him shall not perish but have eternal life.

variant_signals.keyword: only begotten / only / one and only
variant_signals.impact: The rendering of monogenes affects whether the emphasis is on uniqueness or biological begetting.

step2_translations.impact_summary: The key translation variant centers on monogenes — whether 'only begotten' (KJV) or 'only/one and only' (ESV/NIV). This affects the theological nuance of the Son's unique relationship to the Father.

usage_examples.snippet: God demonstrates his own love for us

keywords.meaning_in_context: God's sacrificial, self-giving love directed toward the whole world

usage_examples.snippet: the glory of the only begotten from the Father
Abraham offered up his only begotten son Isaac

keywords.meaning_in_context: The unique, one-of-a-kind Son who shares the Father's nature

usage_examples.snippet: if you believe in your heart

keywords.meaning_in_context: Active, personal trust in Jesus — not mere intellectual assent but relational dependence

edges.snippet: God demonstrates his own love for us in this: While we were still sinners, Christ died for us.
God sent his one and only Son into the world that we might live through him.
Take your son, your only son Isaac, whom you love.

clusters.name: God's Love for the World
clusters.rationale: Passages that directly parallel God's sacrificial love expressed toward humanity

clusters.name: The Only Son Typology
clusters.rationale: Passages connecting the sacrifice of an only son to God's gift of Christ

clusters.name: Salvation by Faith
clusters.rationale: Passages developing the theme of believing/trusting for eternal life

principles.statement: God's love is initiating and sacrificial
principles.grounding: The text states God loved first and gave His Son — the initiative and cost are God's.

principles.statement: Salvation is received through trust, not earned through works
principles.grounding: The condition for receiving eternal life is 'believes in him' — personal trust, not performance.

prompts.text: What does it mean that God loved 'the world' — not just a select group? How does this shape my understanding of God's character?
In what area of my life am I trying to earn God's favor rather than trusting in what He has already given?
Father, help me to grasp the width and depth of your love that moved you to give your only Son for me.
How can our community reflect God's initiating love by reaching out to those who feel unworthy or excluded?
